title: Going to the Doctor
summary: A teenager describes a visit to the doctor after feeling unwell for a few days.
body: Last week I did not feel well. My throat was sore and my head hurt. My mum said I needed to see a doctor. We went to the clinic on Monday morning. There were many people in the waiting room. I sat and waited for twenty minutes. Then a nurse called my name. She took me to see the doctor. The doctor was kind and friendly. He asked me many questions about my symptoms. He looked at my throat with a small light. He said my throat was red and a little swollen. He told me to rest at home for two days. He also gave me some medicine to take twice a day. The medicine was small white tablets. He said to drink a lot of water. I also needed to eat warm soup and soft foods. My mum paid for the medicine at the front desk. We went home after that. I took the medicine after lunch and after dinner. After two days, I felt much better. I went back to school on Wednesday. I was happy to see my friends again.

annotations.exampleEn: We went to the clinic near our house.
There were five people in the waiting room.
The doctor asked about her symptoms.
His ankle was swollen after the fall.
She took her medicine every morning.
The doctor prescribed small white tablets.
Take this medicine twice a day, after meals.
The doctor told him to rest for three days.
She paid at the front desk before leaving.
After two days of rest, she felt much better.
My mum made warm soup when I was sick.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past tense for completed actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Simple past dùng cho các hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ: 'I did not feel well.' / 'The doctor asked me many questions.' Thêm '-ed' vào động từ thường, hoặc dùng dạng bất quy tắc như 'went', 'said', 'took'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple past describes completed actions in the past: 'I did not feel well.' / 'The doctor asked me many questions.' Add '-ed' to regular verbs, or use irregular forms like 'went', 'said', 'took'.

grammarNotes.title: Told + object + infinitive
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Told + object + to + infinitive' dùng để nói ai đó bảo bạn làm gì: 'He told me to rest at home.' Cấu trúc này khác với 'said': không thể nói 'He said me to rest'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Told + object + to-infinitive' reports what someone instructed: 'He told me to rest at home.' This differs from 'said' — you cannot say 'He said me to rest'.

translationVi: Tuần trước tôi không cảm thấy khỏe. Họng tôi bị đau và đầu tôi nhức. Mẹ tôi nói tôi cần đi khám bác sĩ. Chúng tôi đến phòng khám vào sáng thứ Hai. Có rất nhiều người trong phòng chờ. Tôi ngồi và chờ hai mươi phút. Sau đó một y tá gọi tên tôi. Cô ấy đưa tôi đến gặp bác sĩ. Bác sĩ rất tốt bụng và thân thiện. Ông ấy hỏi tôi nhiều câu hỏi về các triệu chứng của tôi. Ông ấy dùng một chiếc đèn nhỏ để xem họng tôi. Ông ấy nói họng tôi bị đỏ và hơi sưng. Ông ấy bảo tôi nghỉ ngơi ở nhà hai ngày. Ông ấy cũng cho tôi một ít thuốc uống hai lần một ngày. Thuốc là những viên nhỏ màu trắng. Ông ấy nói tôi phải uống nhiều nước. Tôi cũng cần ăn súp nóng và thức ăn mềm. Mẹ tôi trả tiền thuốc ở quầy lễ tân. Sau đó chúng tôi về nhà. Tôi uống thuốc sau bữa trưa và sau bữa tối. Sau hai ngày, tôi cảm thấy khá hơn nhiều. Tôi đến trường trở lại vào thứ Tư. Tôi rất vui khi gặp lại bạn bè.

questions.prompt: What was wrong with the narrator last week?
questions.explanation: 'My throat was sore and my head hurt.'
questions.hint: Look at the first few sentences.

questions.prompt: When did they go to the clinic?
questions.explanation: 'We went to the clinic on Monday morning.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence mentioning the day.

questions.prompt: How long did the narrator wait before seeing the doctor?
questions.explanation: 'I sat and waited for twenty minutes.'
questions.hint: Look for the sentence about waiting.
Find the sentence about being called.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator describe the doctor?
questions.hint: Look for the narrator's description of the doctor.

questions.prompt: What did the doctor use to look at the narrator's throat?
questions.explanation: 'He looked at my throat with a small light.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about checking the throat.

questions.prompt: What did the doctor say the throat looked like?
questions.explanation: 'He said my throat was red and a little swollen.'
questions.hint: Find the doctor's diagnosis about the throat.

questions.prompt: How many days did the doctor tell the narrator to rest at home?
questions.explanation: 'He told me to rest at home for two days.'

questions.prompt: How often did the narrator need to take the medicine?
questions.explanation: 'He also gave me some medicine to take twice a day.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about medicine dosage.
Find the description of the tablets.

questions.prompt: What day did the narrator go back to school?
questions.explanation: 'I went back to school on Wednesday.'
questions.hint: Look near the end of the passage.

questions.prompt: What food did the doctor recommend?
questions.explanation: 'I also needed to eat warm soup and soft foods.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about food advice.

blanks.explanation: 'Last week I did not feel well.'
'My throat was sore and my head hurt.'
'My mum said I needed to see a doctor.'
'We went to the clinic on Monday morning.'
'There were many people in the waiting room.'
'He asked me many questions about my symptoms.'
'He said my throat was red and a little swollen.'

'He said to drink a lot of water.'
'After two days, I felt much better.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator got better after following the doctor's advice. Why is it important to listen to a doctor and take your medicine properly, even when you start feeling better?
